name: @solana/mosaic-sdk
description: SDK for Mosaic with helper functions for issuance and management of any token

_dependencyNotes.solanaKitV6: This SDK targets the Solana Kit v6 ecosystem (@solana/kit@6.10, @solana/sysvars@6.10). Kit is capped at 6.x (not 7.x) because the token-acl SDKs peer @solana/kit ^6.0.0. @solana-program/token-2022 is the published 0.12.0, PATCHED via pnpm.patchedDependencies (see root patches/@solana-program__token-2022@0.12.0.patch) to add the confidential mint/burn InstructionPlan helpers (getConfidentialMintInstructionPlan/getConfidentialBurnInstructionPlan). A git-tracked patch (not a vendored .tgz) so it applies on CI/Vercel with --frozen-lockfile. INTERIM: the published 0.13.0 (npm latest) does NOT let us drop this patch — it ships no mint/burn InstructionPlan helpers AND peers @solana/kit ^7 (conflicts with the kit-v6-capped token-acl SDKs below). The patch already carries token-2022 PR #1269 (getConfidentialTransferInstructionPlan derives the auditor from an optional mintAccount). Dropping the patch and bumping needs BOTH: (a) the mint/burn helpers upstreamed into a published token-2022, and (b) a token-acl release supporting kit v7. On 0.11+ the confidential helpers/derives (getConfidentialTransferInstructionPlan, deriveAeKeyForOwnerMint, etc.) live on the @solana-program/token-2022/confidential subpath (moved off the root barrel, not removed); the confidential module imports them from there. @solana/token-acl-sdk@0.4.0 depends on token-2022 0.12.0 and resolves against this patched 0.12.0 — safe because it only uses stable core exports (TOKEN_2022_PROGRAM_ADDRESS, getMintDecoder, ATA/metadata helpers, AccountState, Mint) and the patch only adds to the /confidential entry. @solana-program/zk-elgamal-proof@0.1.0 declares a kit-v5 peer but runs on v6 (only codecs/signers/addresses, unchanged v5->v6); the root overrides force it onto v6. See: https://github.com/anza-xyz/kit
